localeNote: Canonical user-facing copy is English. Future translations must not alter archetype ids, preset names, or layer names.
frontendSurfacePresetRule: feature-sliced when src/app, src/pages, src/features, src/entities, or src/shared exists; otherwise layered

crud-product.label: Product with UI and stored data
crud-product.analogy: A restaurant: recipes (domain), kitchen coordinator (application), waiters (presentation), suppliers (adapters).
antiPatterns: Database client or ORM imports inside domain/
HTTP or fetch calls inside business rules

books.title: Clean Architecture
books.author: Martin
books.for: why business rules stay independent of UI and database

api-backend.label: API server without UI in this repository
api-backend.analogy: A kitchen that only serves through a window: rules and coordination inside, orders in and out through adapters.
antiPatterns: SQL or ORM calls mixed into route handlers without a repository layer
Domain rules that know HTTP status codes

frontend-surface.label: UI-focused repository; backend lives elsewhere or is thin
frontend-surface.analogy: A storefront display: what users see and click; inventory and payments happen behind the counter elsewhere.
antiPatterns: Embedding database access in UI components
Business rules scattered across page files

books.title: Feature-Sliced Design
books.author: FSD community
books.for: scaling a large UI codebase by feature slices

library-sdk.label: Publishable package others import
library-sdk.analogy: A toolbox: clear public tools on top, internal parts hidden, no storefront of its own.
antiPatterns: Leaking framework-specific types as the only public API
Hidden global state initialized on import

cli-utility.label: Command-line tool
cli-utility.analogy: A vending machine panel: commands in, results out; storage and network are plug-ins behind the panel.
antiPatterns: Business logic tangled with argv parsing and console output
Direct filesystem or network calls without a testable core

books.title: Clean Architecture
books.author: Martin
books.for: separating use cases from delivery mechanisms

worker-pipeline.label: Background jobs and queues without a user-facing UI
worker-pipeline.analogy: A night shift factory line: triggers arrive, work gets done, results stored — no shop floor for customers.
antiPatterns: Job handlers that duplicate use-case logic from HTTP routes
Unbounded retries without idempotency at the domain boundary

event-coordinator.label: Long-running or multi-step business processes
event-coordinator.analogy: A wedding planner: many steps, compensations when something fails, state tracked across days.
antiPatterns: Saga logic spread across unrelated HTTP handlers
Missing compensation when an external step fails

integration-bridge.label: Connects external systems with thin domain logic
integration-bridge.analogy: A translator at a border: messages in, messages out, minimal local rules.
antiPatterns: Copy-paste integration code per webhook instead of shared adapters
Secrets and env access outside a dedicated config surface

multi-app-workspace.label: Several deployable units in one repository
multi-app-workspace.analogy: A mall with separate shops sharing a parking lot: each app is a tenant, shared packages are utilities.
antiPatterns: One package importing another app's internal directories
A single catch-all layer that hides cross-package coupling

prototype-spike.label: Fast experiment or learning project
prototype-spike.analogy: A sketch before the painting: enough structure to learn, expand when the idea stabilizes.
antiPatterns: Adopting every layer on day one before the product shape is known
Skipping governance entirely because the repo is small

vertical-slice-product.label: Feature-first product (vertical slices)
vertical-slice-product.analogy: A food truck menu: each dish (feature) is cooked end-to-end; shared utensils stay in the common drawer — never borrow another dish's mise en place.
antiPatterns: Importing code from features/A into features/B
Dumping business rules into shared/ just to avoid a cross-slice import

books.title: Vertical Slice Architecture
books.author: Jimmy Bogard (community)
books.for: organizing by use-case instead of technical layer only

ddd-bounded-contexts.label: Multiple business domains (bounded contexts)
ddd-bounded-contexts.analogy: Separate city districts: each has its own shops and rules; only the shared square (kernel) and mail (events) connect them — never dig tunnels between private basements.
antiPatterns: One context importing another context's domain model
A god shared/ folder that re-exports every context

books.title: Implementing Domain-Driven Design
books.author: Vernon
books.for: bounded contexts, shared kernel, and integration patterns
